changes_made: Renamed k8s_audit_logging_enabled to k8s_audit_logging_configured for clarity
review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security controls relevant to SOC2 CC7.3, which focuses on evaluating and responding to security events. However, minor naming adjustments were made for clarity and specificity. Audit logging and admission controllers are correctly identified as key components for detecting and responding to security events. Network policies are also relevant but require clearer naming to reflect their monitoring role.
naming_improvements: Ensure function names clearly describe the specific security feature or action being checked.
title: CC7.3 The entity evaluates security events to determine whether they could or have resulted in a failure of the entity to meet its objectives (security incidents) and, if so, takes actions to prevent or address such failures
step1_reasoning: The SOC2 control CC7.3 focuses on evaluating security events and taking action to prevent or address failures. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that audit logging is enabled and properly configured to capture security events. Additionally, admission controllers can be configured to detect and respond to security events at the time of resource creation or modification. NetworkPolicies can be used to monitor and control network traffic, which is crucial for detecting potential security incidents. These mappings ensure that Kubernetes clusters can evaluate and respond to security events effectively.
